pi_items.source: MoodleDocs Timeline_block and HTML_block documentation; task.json description; verifier.py checks for both block types; export_result.sh checks for 'timeline' and 'html' blockname values

MoodleDocs Blocks FAQ confirms online_users as standard Moodle block; task.json description line 3 states 'Remove the Online users block if present'; verifier.py line 47 checks online_users_found; export_result.sh line 61 checks blockname === 'online_users'

task.json description explicitly states title should be 'Student Support'; export_result.sh line 74 checks stripos($config->title, 'Student Support'); verifier.py line 65 checks support_block_title_correct

task.json description states content should be 'For technical assistance, please contact helpdesk@university.edu or call 555-0199'; export_result.sh line 83 checks for 'helpdesk@university.edu' in block content; verifier.py line 79 checks support_block_content_correct

setup_task.sh lines 10-26 create/verify user jsmith and save ID to /tmp/jsmith_id.txt; lines 28-41 create custom dashboard record for jsmith to test reset functionality; export_result.sh lines 13-102 check if jsmith's custom dashboard still exists using the saved user ID

privileged_info_summary: This task configures the system-wide default Moodle dashboard using real Moodle block types: Timeline (a standard Moodle block providing deadline overviews) and HTML (standard Text block for rich content). The task removes the Online Users block (a standard Moodle monitoring block) and adds a support block titled 'Student Support' with contact information 'helpdesk@university.edu'. The verification uses synthetic test data including a fake user account 'jsmith' to confirm the dashboard reset functionality works correctly by checking that user-specific customizations are deleted when the system-wide reset is triggered. All block type names and database queries are consistent with Moodle 4.0+ architecture using pagetypepattern 'my-index' for dashboard block queries.
